title: Caduceus External Perturbational Regulatory Generalization Memory

resource_records.name: DC-TAP matched distal perturbational candidate
resource_records.source: Constructed inside the fresh Caduceus SPECTRA session from public/local distal enhancer-gene perturbational resources.
resource_records.access_notes: Most reusable as a hypothesis-test template. Reconstruct from the portable dataset catalog and revalidate source provenance before using in a new paper run.

resource_records.name: CRISPR comparison perturbational companions
resource_records.source: Constructed as source-aware and source/sequence-bin matched companion tests in the same fresh Caduceus session.
resource_records.access_notes: Use as controls against overgeneralizing the DC-TAP curve into a broad pretrained-representation mechanism.

similarity_axes.name: distal_element_gene_distance_bin
similarity_axes.definition: Compare 10-to-100 kb versus greater-than-or-equal-to-100 kb element-gene candidate bins after matching label, cell type, chromatin category, assay power, target-gene ubiquity, and measured 2048 bp sequence overlap.
quality_gates: Measured 2048 bp train-eval overlap decreases from the nearer to the farther bin.
Controls-only model is reported beside Caduceus-plus-controls.
Do not use tested_negative_high_power as a predictor because it is label-definition leakage.

similarity_axes.name: source_aware_crispr_distance_companion
similarity_axes.definition: Repeat perturbational distance-bin analysis on CRISPR companion resources with source-aware matching and controls-only comparisons.
quality_gates: Curve support is not generalized unless replicated across companion resources.

findings.claim: Matched DC-TAP exposed a valid distance-conditioned deployment fragility curve: Caduceus plus controls AUROC dropped from 0.861 at 10-to-100 kb to 0.540 at greater-than-or-equal-to-100 kb.
limitations: Frozen 2048 bp element-centered Caduceus embeddings only.
Deployment fragility is not the same as a Caduceus-specific mechanism.

findings.claim: The same experiment did not show stable Caduceus-specific incremental signal beyond explicit controls.
evidence: Caduceus-plus-controls versus controls-only delta AUROC was -0.0018 at 10-to-100 kb.
Caduceus-plus-controls versus controls-only delta AUROC was -0.1123 at greater-than-or-equal-to-100 kb.
CRISPR companions were weak or control-dominated.
limitations: Does not characterize all regulatory DNA tasks or all cell types.
Mechanism closure needs a higher-support resource where provenance and sequence/background bins can be matched without losing positives.

reuse_guidance: For Caduceus-like DNA foundation models, query this memory before constructing external regulatory or perturbational datasets.
Reuse the matched DC-TAP and CRISPR companion design as a template for element-gene-cell-type deployment curves.
Always compare target-model embeddings against controls-only baselines and treat distance as deployment difficulty unless a model-specific increment survives.
Promote higher-support public perturbational resources when the goal is mechanism closure rather than bounded deployment fragility.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat tested_negative_high_power as a feature; it is label-definition leakage.
Do not broaden the DC-TAP result into a claim that Caduceus globally fails regulatory biology.
Do not stop at distance or sequence overlap if the question asks for biological mechanism.
Do not present frozen-probe evidence as full fine-tuning evidence.
